one_time_description: Loss from Alexis owned enterprises (government business enterprises) of $9,009,990 due to significant decline in equity in enterprises from $43.2M to $29.7M
economic_notes: Nation has 6 government business enterprises including Alexis Casino Corporation, Alexis First Nation Business Trust, Alexis Nakota Sioux Nation Business Trust, Alexis Land Management Corporation, Nakota Crossing Ltd., and Hill Plain Construction. GBEs had combined revenue of $36.2M and expenses of $45.5M in 2024 resulting in a net loss of $9.3M. Nation guaranteed two non-revolving loans of Nakota Crossing Ltd. with maximum values of $8,507,000; outstanding balance $5,639,295. Major capital investment: $35M in tangible capital assets primarily housing ($26.6M additions).
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to obtain sufficient audit evidence on equity in Alexis owned enterprises (GBEs) for both 2024 and 2023. Nation has 2 consolidated subsidiaries (Northern ISGA Foundation, Alexis Board of Education) and 6 GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Massive capital asset additions of $35M primarily in housing ($26.6M). Equity in GBEs dropped from $43.2M to $29.7M due to $9M share of losses. CMHC revenue of $26.2M is unusually high compared to prior year $612K suggesting major CMHC project funding received. ISC funding calculated as Block + Flexible + Fixed + Health = $26,792,906. Total government transfers includes ISC ($26,792,906) + CMHC ($26,210,272) + GoA/Federal grants ($2,383,906) + Yellowhead Tribal ($735,698) + Northern ISGA Foundation ($1,761,694) + Settlement Trust ($568,435) = ~$58,452,911 less deferred adjustments; used statement line items summing to $57,905,515 approx. OSR calculated as total revenue $63,669,483 minus government transfers. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. Segment disclosure appears truncated in source document.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
